evidence_quotes.quote: Protecting live service games from being permanently shut down.

paragraphs.text: All of the current content layed out in the Digital Fairness Act is great, and should all be passed. I am here to suggest an additional protection be added to the Act. Protecting live service games from being permanently shut down. These are some amazing video games which are not just that, but pieces of media/art which both people have worked on for thousands of hours, and people have played and enjoyed for thousands of hours. Personally, there are some games which I would consider to be my childhood which I have sunk much of my life into to bond with others, and have a good time. These being closed forever could make it feel like it was all for nothing, and they would be pieces of media history erased. The concensus among many supporters of the Stop Killing Games initiative is that while a company may not have to keep the servers running forever, they should make server code open so people may keep games running themselves. As it stands now, that is almost always not the case, and beloved media which people have sunk money and time into get erased. Please consider amending the act to include the protection of live service games, requiring developers to properly sunset their games so they may be played for years after support ends. Thank you for your time.
